fingerprint: Computational ghost — 2.26× ratio exists ONLY as homepage text with zero code, zero monitoring, zero derivation. monitor.py computes total drift speed via empirical exponential fit to NOAA data but never decomposes into directional components. Dome's own W019 weekly test found 5.5× (not 2.26×) and stayed PENDING. Axisymmetric H(r) structurally cannot predict directional preferences.
globe_value: NOAA NP.xy pole positions reported in geographic latitude/longitude — spherical coordinate system. Directional decomposition into lat/lon components is trigonometry on a sphere. Standard geophysics: Livermore et al. 2020 (Nature Geoscience) explain longitudinal dominance via core-mantle boundary flux lobe competition between Canadian and Siberian lobes — an inherently 3D spherical structure.
dome_value: 2.26× stated as text on homepage ('NMP longitudinal dominance 2.26×'). wins.html: derived from 2020-2025 NOAA data: lon rate −37.1 km/yr, lat rate −16.4 km/yr, ratio = 37.1/16.4 = 2.26×. No dome parameter, formula, or geometry produces this number.
match_precision: N/A — no dome-derived value to compare. The 2.26× is purely observational arithmetic. Closest dome parameter ratio: disc_radius/lambda_g = 20015/8619 = 2.322 (2.8% off, coincidental — no claimed connection).
why_it_matters:
Five converging findings:

1. COMPUTATIONAL GHOST: The 2.26× ratio does not appear in inject_ai_layer.py (confirmed_wins only goes to WIN-026, WIN-043 absent), monitor.py (Domain 3 tracks total drift speed only — formula: rate=55×exp(−0.08×(year−2015)) — no directional decomposition), scoring.js (no reference), verify_predictions.py (no reference), formulas.json (133 formulas, none related to directional ratio), predictions.json (no WIN-043 entry), or parameters.json (no 2.26 value). The number exists exclusively as text on the homepage and in wins.html. This is the same pattern as WIN-042: a display-layer-only claim with zero computational backing.

2. MONITOR.PY KNOWS DIRECTION BUT IGNORES IT: monitor.py's fetch_nmp_history() function DOES extract both latitude and longitude from NOAA NP.xy (lines 213-237). It DOES compute velocity from consecutive points (lines 228-237: y1,lat1,lon1 → y2,lat2,lon2, dt=y2−y1). But it ONLY computes total rate (line 240: rate_pred = 55.0 × exp(−0.08×(year−2015))), NEVER decomposes into lat/lon components, and NEVER computes their ratio. The infrastructure to verify 2.26× is present but unused. Domain 3 checks total speed against a 50% adaptive tolerance; no domain checks the 2.26× ratio.

3. DOME'S OWN W019 CONTRADICTS: The dome's weekly prediction W019 ('NMP drift direction this week: Poleward dominates lateral') found the OPPOSITE: 'lateral 5.5× larger [than poleward]' (from predictions.html W019 REFINED note). This 5.5× is not 2.26×. W019 remained PENDING despite WIN-043 being 'CONFIRMED' — the dome claims the ratio as a confirmed prediction while its own weekly test of directional dominance shows a different ratio and can't confirm it.

4. AXISYMMETRIC IMPOSSIBILITY: The dome's field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) depends only on radial distance r. It is axisymmetric — identical at all azimuths for a given radius. A directional ratio (how much faster the pole moves in longitude vs latitude) requires azimuthally non-uniform field structure. On a globe, this comes from core-mantle boundary flux lobes at specific geographic positions (Livermore 2020). On a flat disc with H(r)-only, there is no mechanism for directional preference — the model predicts isotropic motion. The 2.26× longitudinal dominance structurally cannot be derived from dome geometry.

5. EPOCH-DEPENDENT SNAPSHOT: wins.html computes 2.26× from 2020-2025 velocity components (lon: −37.1, lat: −16.4 km/yr). Over the full 1990-2025 trajectory, the pole moved ~106° in longitude but only ~3.8° in latitude — a naïve ratio of ~28×, not 2.26×. The dome selected a narrow 5-year window that produces a smaller ratio. The NMP has been decelerating since ~2019 (Chulliat et al. 2020, Earth Planets Space 72:27), and the directional balance is shifting. The 2.26× will inevitably change, at which point the dome will either silently update or ignore the discrepancy. A genuine prediction would derive the ratio as a time-dependent function from physical parameters.

files_checked: inject_ai_layer.py — confirmed_wins array stops at WIN-026. No 2.26 value. No WIN-043. Closest numeric: 2.56e-8 (WIN-016 alpha) and 2.66 (WIN-019 analemma).

monitor.py — Domain 3 ('NMP Drift Rate'): rate_pred = 55.0 × exp(−0.08×(year−2015)). Computes TOTAL speed only. fetch_nmp_history() extracts (year,lat,lon) but velocity decomposition into lat/lon components is never performed. Domain 11 ('NMP Longitude'): checks pole lon > 130°E, predicted=139.3. Neither domain computes 2.26× ratio. No 'longitudinal' or '2.26' string anywhere in file.
scoring.js — No WIN-043, no 2.26, no NMP references.
verify_predictions.py — Eclipse computations only. No NMP references.
api/current/formulas.json — 133 formulas. FORM-004 (SAA Node Separation) is closest geomagnetic entry. Zero formulas for NMP directional ratio. No 2.26 value.
api/current/parameters.json — No 2.26 value. No NMP directional parameter.
docs/wins.html — WIN-043 described. 2.26× computed from: lon rate −37.1 km/yr, lat rate −16.4 km/yr over 2020-2025. 'Toroidal Aetheric Vortex Model' cited as mechanism. No formula provided.

docs/predictions.html — PRED-R003 (uses 2.26× to project pole reaching 141-146°E by 2031), PRED-R004 ('NMP Longitudinal Ratio ≥2.0× Through 2028'), DW-002 (weekly tracking log). All reference 2.26× as assumed input, none derive it.
docs/model.html — Toroidal vortex described but no geometric derivation connecting it to 2.26×. W019 REFINED note: 'lateral 5.5× larger' — contradicts 2.26× claim.
code_search.found_in: wins.html (WIN-043 text entry: lon=-37.1, lat=-16.4, ratio=2.26 from 2020-2025 NOAA data), predictions.html (PRED-R003/R004/DW-002 reference 2.26× as assumed value)
code_search.notes: The 2.26× exists in THREE dome contexts: (1) homepage text claim, (2) wins.html observational arithmetic, (3) downstream predictions (PRED-R003/R004/DW-002) that ASSUME 2.26× as input. Zero derivation from dome geometry anywhere. monitor.py has the infrastructure to verify it (fetch_nmp_history extracts lat/lon) but never does. The dome's own W019 test found 5.5× lateral dominance over a weekly window, contradicting the claimed 2.26× annual ratio.

cross_references: WIN-006/007/036 fingerprints: Same NOAA NP.xy data dependency, same globe-coordinate system assumption
WIN-040 fingerprint: Same axisymmetric impossibility for longitude-dependent claims
WIN-042 fingerprint: Same computational ghost pattern (display-layer-only with zero code backing)
WIN-059/060/061 fingerprints (if reviewed): Same NMP cluster, same data source
suggested_text: The 2.26× ratio does not appear in any computational file in the dome repository: not in inject_ai_layer.py, monitor.py, scoring.js, formulas.json, or parameters.json. monitor.py's fetch_nmp_history() extracts latitude and longitude from NOAA NP.xy and computes total drift speed (Domain 3: rate = 55 × exp(−0.08 × (year − 2015))), but never decomposes velocity into directional components or computes their ratio. The 2.26× is a text-only assertion derived from a 5-year window (2020–2025) of NOAA data: lon rate −37.1 km/yr ÷ lat rate −16.4 km/yr. Computing over the full 1990–2025 trajectory yields a ratio far larger than 2.26×, confirming the value is epoch-specific. The dome's own weekly directional test (W019) found 5.5× lateral dominance — a different value entirely — and remained PENDING while WIN-043 was marked CONFIRMED.
